raw: {"perspective": "Scaling Engineer", "findings": [{"severity": "medium", "file_or_area": "CEO budget allocation", "issue": "Operating at $100/month creates hard infrastructure ceilings. At 10x users, Postgres RDS will exhaust memory on analytics queries, Supabase function timeouts will spike, and API latency will breach 2s baseline. No buffer for incident response or scaling bursts.", "recommended_fix": "Pre-negotiate scaling triggers with AWS/Supabase. Allocate 30% of next month's budget to reserved RDS instances and connection pooling. Map database growth against user count now, not at crisis."}, {"severity": "high", "file_or_area": "swarm/agents/", "issue": "Swarm architecture expects 13 active perspectives, but directory is empty. At scale, failure in one agent will cascade — no isolation, no fallback. System treats swarm as functional when it's symbolic.", "recommended_fix": "Implement at least 3 core agents (Security, QA, Needs) as independent runtimes with health checks. Decouple from Atlas' execution thread. Test failure isolation before user growth."}], "overall_verdict": "warn", "whistleblower_flag": null}

findings.issue: Operating at $100/month creates hard infrastructure ceilings. At 10x users, Postgres RDS will exhaust memory on analytics queries, Supabase function timeouts will spike, and API latency will breach 2s baseline. No buffer for incident response or scaling bursts.
findings.recommended_fix: Pre-negotiate scaling triggers with AWS/Supabase. Allocate 30% of next month's budget to reserved RDS instances and connection pooling. Map database growth against user count now, not at crisis.

findings.issue: Swarm architecture expects 13 active perspectives, but directory is empty. At scale, failure in one agent will cascade — no isolation, no fallback. System treats swarm as functional when it's symbolic.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement at least 3 core agents (Security, QA, Needs) as independent runtimes with health checks. Decouple from Atlas' execution thread. Test failure isolation before user growth.
